subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Hero Components (Core Set)
- **5 heroes** — Spider-Man, Captain Marvel, She-Hulk, Iron Man, Black Panther.
- **Hero cards** — Each hero has a double-sided identity card (hero/alter-ego) and a unique 15-card hero deck.
- **Obligation cards** — 1 per hero, shuffled into encounter deck.
- **Nemesis sets** — Each hero has a nemesis mini-set that can enter play.

#### Player Deck Cards
- **4 aspects** — Aggression (red), Justice (yellow), Leadership (blue), Protection (green).
- **Basic cards** — Neutral cards usable by any hero.
- **Card types** — Allies, Events, Upgrades, Supports, Resources.

#### Villain Components
- **3 villain sets** — Rhino, Klaw, Ultron (core set).
- **Villain cards** — Multi-stage villain with escalating stats.
- **Main scheme cards** — Multi-stage scheme the villain advances.
- **Encounter sets** — Modular encounter sets mixed into villain deck.

#### Tokens & Trackers
- **Damage tokens** — Track damage on villain, allies, and heroes.
- **Threat tokens** — Track threat on schemes.
- **Status cards** — Stunned, Confused, Tough.
- **Acceleration tokens** — Increase threat placed per round.
- **Hit point dials/trackers** — Track hero and villain HP.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose villain** — Select villain set and set up stage 1 villain card.
2. **Set villain HP** — Multiply per-player HP value by number of players.
3. **Place main scheme** — Put stage 1A of main scheme face up; place starting threat as indicated.
4. **Build encounter deck** — Combine villain-specific cards + required encounter sets + 1 modular encounter set.
5. **Shuffle obligations** — Add each hero's obligation card to encounter deck and shuffle.
6. **Place tokens** — Set damage, threat, and status tokens in central supply.
7. **First player token** — Give to a randomly chosen player.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Deck Construction
- **Deck size** — 40-50 cards (including hero's 15-card kit).
- **Aspect choice** — Choose 1 aspect; include aspect cards and basic cards to reach 40-50 total.
- **Hero kit** — The 15 hero-specific cards are always included.
- **No duplicates beyond limit** — Maximum 3 copies of any non-unique card.

#### At the Table
- **Identity card** — Place hero identity card alter-ego side up.
- **Set HP** — Set hit point tracker to printed maximum.
- **Draw hand** — Draw cards equal to alter-ego hand size (typically 6).
- **Mulligan** — Shuffle any number of cards back, draw replacements (one-time).
- **Nemesis set aside** — Place hero's nemesis set aside, out of play.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **All start in alter-ego** — Heroes begin in alter-ego form.
2. **Resolve setup instructions** — Follow any "Setup" text on scheme or villain cards.
3. **First player begins** — The first player takes their hero turn.
4. **No villain phase on round 1** — Some groups skip the first villain phase; official rules include it.
5. **Play proceeds clockwise** — Each player takes a full hero turn before the villain phase.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Player Phase** — Each player takes a complete hero turn in clockwise order.
2. **Villain Phase** — The villain activates against each player, then encounter cards are dealt.

#### Hero Turn
- **Play cards** — Play allies, upgrades, supports, and events from hand by paying resource cost.
- **Use abilities** — Activate hero, ally, and card abilities.
- **Generate resources** — Discard cards from hand to generate resources (each card = its printed resource icons).
- **Basic powers** — Use ATK (attack villain), THW (thwart scheme), DEF (defend), or REC (recover in alter-ego).
- **Change form** — Flip identity card once per turn (hero ↔ alter-ego). Exhausts the identity card.

#### Villain Phase (in order)
1. **Place threat** — Add threat to main scheme equal to acceleration value (1 base + acceleration tokens).
2. **Villain activates** — Against each player: if in hero form, villain attacks; if in alter-ego, villain schemes (adds threat).
3. **Deal encounter cards** — Each player draws 1 encounter card (2 in alter-ego) and resolves it.
4. **Pass first player** — Move first player token clockwise.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Basic Powers (exhaust identity card)
- **Attack (ATK)** — In hero form: deal ATK value as damage to villain.
- **Thwart (THW)** — In hero form: remove THW value of threat from a scheme.
- **Defend (DEF)** — In hero form: exhaust to defend against villain attack, reducing damage by DEF value.
- **Recover (REC)** — In alter-ego form: heal HP equal to REC value.

#### Card Types
- **Allies** — Enter play with HP; can attack, thwart, or defend. Take 1 consequential damage per use.
- **Upgrades** — Attach to hero; provide ongoing bonuses or abilities.
- **Supports** — Enter play area; provide resources, card draw, or abilities.
- **Events** — One-time effects; discard after resolving.
- **Resources** — Cards with wild or specific resource icons used for paying costs.

#### Paying Costs
- **Resource generation** — Discard cards from hand; each card generates its printed resource icons.
- **Double resources** — Some cards generate 2 icons of a specific type.
- **Wild resources** — Wild icons count as any resource type.

#### Defending
- **Declare defender** — When villain attacks, you may exhaust hero or an ally to defend.
- **Undefended damage** — If no defender, all damage is dealt to hero's HP.
- **Ally defense** — Ally takes damage instead; excess does not spill over to hero.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Form Flipping
- **Hero form** — Can attack and thwart; villain attacks you during villain phase.
- **Alter-ego form** — Can recover HP; villain schemes during villain phase; draw more encounter cards.
- **Flip cost** — Flipping exhausts your identity card (uses your basic power for the turn).
- **Strategic tension** — Staying hero keeps scheme pressure down but risks HP; alter-ego heals but lets threat build.

#### Villain Stages
- **Multi-stage villain** — When a villain stage is reduced to 0 HP, advance to the next stage.
- **Stage HP** — Each stage has its own HP value (scaled by player count).
- **Final stage** — Defeating the final stage wins the game.

#### Scheme Advancement
- **Threat accumulation** — Threat builds on main scheme each villain phase and from scheme encounter cards.
- **Scheme threshold** — If threat reaches the scheme's threshold, the scheme advances.
- **Final scheme stage** — If the last scheme stage completes, the villain wins.

#### Status Cards
- **Stunned** — Next time the character would attack or thwart, discard Stun instead (no effect).
- **Confused** — Next time the character would thwart or scheme, discard Confused instead.
- **Tough** — Next time the character would take damage, discard Tough instead (prevent all damage from that source).

#### Encounter Cards
- **Treacheries** — Resolve immediately; apply effects then discard.
- **Minions** — Enter play engaged with the player; must be dealt with like a mini-villain.
- **Attachments** — Attach to villain or scheme, enhancing their effects.
- **Side schemes** — Secondary schemes that add threat pressure and often block the main scheme.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Defeat the villain** — Reduce the villain's final stage to 0 HP.

#### Loss Conditions
- **Scheme completes** — The main scheme's final stage reaches its threat threshold.
- **All heroes defeated** — All players' heroes are reduced to 0 HP.
- **Individual defeat** — A defeated hero is eliminated; remaining heroes continue.

#### Scoring
- **No VP system** — Marvel Champions is a binary win/loss cooperative game.
- **Difficulty scaling** — Standard, Expert, and Heroic modes increase challenge.
- **Expert mode** — Use stage 2 of the villain from the start; adds expert encounter set.
- **Heroic mode** — Deal additional encounter cards each villain phase.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Set up key upgrades early** — Play resource generators and key hero upgrades in the first 1-2 turns.
- **Start in alter-ego** — Use the higher alter-ego hand size for setup; flip to hero once established.
- **Coordinate aspect roles** — Justice handles threat, Aggression pushes damage, Protection tanks, Leadership builds allies.
- **Manage threat from turn 1** — Even during setup, monitor main scheme threat to avoid early scheme advances.
- **Play allies early** — Allies provide extra actions each turn and can absorb villain attacks.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring threat** — Focusing only on damage while scheme threat quietly reaches the threshold.
- **Staying in alter-ego too long** — Extra encounter cards and villain scheming can spiral out of control.
- **Wasting allies** — Using allies for consequential-damage attacks when they could defend instead.
- **Over-defending** — Exhausting hero to defend every attack prevents you from attacking or thwarting on your turn.
- **Poor resource management** — Discarding high-value cards for resources when cheaper options exist.
- **Solo neglecting Justice** — Without a thwart-focused aspect, solo games often lose to scheme advancement.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Hero vs alter-ego timing** — When to flip for recovery vs staying in hero form to manage threats.
- **Damage vs thwart priority** — Read the board state to decide if reducing villain HP or removing threat is more urgent.
- **Defend vs take the hit** — Whether to exhaust hero/ally to defend or absorb damage to keep actions available.
- **Target minions vs villain** — Clear minions to reduce incoming damage or focus fire on the villain.
- **Resource vs play** — Whether a card is worth more played for its effect or discarded for its resource icons.
